title: Aurora Cannabis ($ACB) Is Building A Medical Export Engine, Not Waiting For U.S. Legalization
text: Merlin Trader analysis argues Aurora Cannabis building medical export engine independent of U.S. legalization. Strategic positioning: Aurora increasingly positioned as global medical cannabis supplier rather than domestic U.S. consumer brand. EU GMP certification and manufacturing infrastructure enabling supply to multiple regulated markets. Medical focus: Company emphasized medical-first strategy prioritizing high-margin medical products over consumer cannabis. Gross margins in medical segment 69-74% versus 26-40% in consumer and propagation. International markets: Aurora now generates 60% of medical cannabis revenue outside Canada in Q3 2025. Targets include Germany, Australia, Poland, UK with strong market positions. Germany opportunity: Post-legalization, German market experiencing double-digit patient growth. Aurora benefiting from market expansion and competitive positioning. Manufacturing advantage: EU GMP certifications provide regulatory advantage in European markets. Comparable to FDA manufacturing standards in pharmaceutical industry. TGA certifications enable Australian market expansion. Risk mitigation: Aurora not dependent on U.S. legalization timeline for growth trajectory. Reduces regulatory and political risk exposure to U.S. Congress. International market opportunity size substantial without U.S. market. Profitability path: International medical margins support path to sustained profitability. Adjusted EBITDA generation demonstrating business model works. Free cash flow positive on full-year basis. Investment implication: Aurora positioned to benefit from global medical cannabis opportunity independent of U.S. legalization.
